channel.title: Visual Studio Code on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Visual Studio Code on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-July 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL added Shortcut Configuration in Visual Studio Code preview, allowing users to customize keyboard shortcuts for Quick Queries, the Results Grid, and the Query Editor. The update is listed as a public preview for mid-July 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, June 2026 releases
item.description:
This changelog covers GitHub Copilot updates in Visual Studio Code across VS Code v1.123 through v1.127, released during June and early July 2026. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code (June Build 2026 refresh)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code is now generally available with a Build 2026 refresh. The update adds the full model catalog, model playground, and Hosted agent deployment directly in the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Introducing AI Assistant in the Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code now includes a public preview AI Assistant. It lets users ask questions in plain English to generate optimized Azure Cosmos DB queries and use chat participant commands.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Relational Database to Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL Migration Assistant
item.description:
The Azure Cosmos DB extension for Visual Studio Code now includes a Public Preview Migration Assistant for moving from relational databases to Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL. It supports migrations from Oracle, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and MySQL and provides a guided workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension in Visual Studio Code
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB Migration Extension for Visual Studio Code is now generally available. It lets you create, run, and manage migration jobs from VS Code to move data from on-premises or cloud-based MongoDB sources.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for June
item.description:
Azure SQL added support in the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code to run SQL queries in SQL Notebooks, document notebooks with Markdown, and combine kernels such as Python with SQL. The update is generally available as of June 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now generally available includes Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It supports guided generation of REST, GraphQL, and Model Context Protocol back ends in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description:
GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension is now generally available in Visual Studio Code. It adds AI-assisted capabilities to the visual schema design experience.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Oracle schema conversion to Azure PostgreSQL in Visual Studio Code
item.description:
The PostgreSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now supports converting Oracle database schema objects into Azure Database for PostgreSQL-compatible schema. The workflow is project-based and runs directly in VS Code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub
